data.headline: Bathroom Remodeling in Orange, CT
data.subheadline: Licensed Connecticut bathroom remodeling contractor serving Orange and New Haven County , complete renovations from walk-in showers to full master suites.

data.title: Bathroom Remodeling in Orange, CT
data.content:
Orange is our home base , we operate from 206A Boston Post Road. The housing stock is primarily split-levels, colonials, capes, and ranch homes built between the 1950s and 1980s. Many of these bathrooms are original to the home , pink, green, or blue tile, small vanities, and tub-shower combos with fiberglass surrounds.

As Orange's local bathroom remodeling contractor, we've completed numerous bathroom renovations within minutes of our office. We know this housing stock and its plumbing conditions better than anyone.

data.title: What Is Included in a Bathroom Remodel in Orange
data.content:
A bathroom remodel in Orange covers demolition, waterproofing, tile, plumbing fixtures, vanity, electrical, ventilation, and all Connecticut permits, handled as a single project from initial assessment through final walkthrough. Orange's 1950s and 1970s ranch homes were typically built with one full bathroom serving the whole house, and converting to a two-bath layout is one of the most common projects we complete in this town.

We begin with full demolition of all existing tile, fixtures, vanity, and flooring, then assess the subfloor, framing, and plumbing before any new materials are selected. Orange ranch homes built on slab require saw-cutting concrete to route new drain lines for a second bathroom, a process that also means surveying the slab for existing electrical conduit runs before the first cut is made. We complete that assessment as part of the pre-construction phase so there are no surprises once concrete is opened.

Waterproofing is installed behind all shower and tub surfaces using sheet membrane systems, with cement board throughout all wet zones. In slab-on-grade Orange bathrooms, the floor waterproofing assembly extends across the full shower pan area and is inspected before tile installation begins, because access to correct a membrane failure on a slab bathroom is substantially more disruptive than it is in a framed floor.

Finish work includes floor and wall tile, vanity and countertop installation, all plumbing fixture connections, electrical updates for lighting and GFCI outlets, and exhaust fan installation vented to the exterior. Orange two-bath conversion projects often involve adding a primary bath adjacent to the main bedroom and repurposing the original hall bath as a shared secondary bathroom, which changes the fixture selections and layout priorities for both rooms. Permit coordination and a final walkthrough are standard on every project.

data.title: What Makes Bathroom Remodeling in Orange Different
data.content:
Orange homeowners are practical and value-oriented. They want honest assessments of what their bathroom renovation will cost, how long it will take, and what's included. As a local Orange business, we deliver exactly that.

Split-level homes , extremely common in Orange , present specific bathroom challenges. The main bathroom is typically on the upper level with a powder room on the main level and potentially a basement half-bath. Plumbing stack locations and joist direction affect layout options. We've renovated dozens of Orange split-level bathrooms and know exactly what to expect.

data.title: Orange Building Department and Permits
data.content: The Orange Building Department at Town Hall on Orange Center Road handles permits efficiently. Bathroom renovations involving plumbing or electrical work require permits. As a local Orange business, we have a strong working relationship with the building department. Permit turnaround is typically 1-2 weeks , faster than most surrounding towns.

data.title: Bathroom Remodeling Cost in Orange, CT
data.subtitle: Bathroom remodeling costs in Orange depend on the scope of work, material selections, and the condition of existing plumbing and structure. Ranges are illustrative for typical Connecticut projects. Final pricing depends on scope, materials, site conditions, and permit fees. Detailed written estimate provided before any work begins.

data.title: Why Bathroom Remodeling in Orange Requires Local Expertise
data.content:
Orange homes present bathroom conditions that contractors without local experience regularly miss.

Orange bathrooms present familiar conditions: cast-iron drain lines ready for replacement, galvanized supply pipes, original tile over plaster, and undersized exhaust fans that have allowed moisture damage to develop for decades.

Split-level homes in Orange have plumbing stacks in specific locations that determine layout options for bathroom renovations. We know where these stacks are in common Orange floor plans and design around them efficiently.

Our proximity means fast response times and familiarity with every Orange neighborhood's construction patterns.

data.title: Our Bathroom Remodeling Process
data.subtitle: Every bathroom remodel follows the same five-step process , structured to keep projects on schedule, on budget, and clearly communicated throughout.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.description: We visit your home, assess the existing bathroom conditions , plumbing, electrical, ventilation, structural , and discuss your goals. No charge. No obligation. We identify potential issues upfront so there are no surprises during construction.

steps.title: Planning
steps.description: You receive a clear written proposal covering every trade: tile, plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, vanity, fixtures, glass, and paint. Each line item is broken out so you understand exactly where the budget is going. We include a complete project schedule.

steps.title: Selections
steps.description: We guide you through tile, vanity, fixture, and hardware selections with options at different price points. We communicate lead times clearly so everything arrives before construction begins. If a material has a long lead time, we flag that immediately.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Construction begins on the agreed schedule. Demolition, rough plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, tile, vanity installation, fixture connections, and paint , all sequenced properly. You receive daily updates and a clean, dust-controlled workspace.

steps.title: Walkthrough
steps.description: We walk through the finished bathroom together , every tile, every grout line, every fixture, every piece of hardware. If anything needs attention, we address it before calling the project complete.

data.title: Project Timeline
data.subtitle: Most bathroom remodels take three to eight weeks from demolition to final walkthrough.

items.title: Planning & Design
items.description: 1–2 Weeks. Consultation, measurements, material selections, and detailed proposal.

items.title: Material Lead Time
items.description: 1–4 Weeks. Vanity, tile, fixtures, and glass enclosure ordering and delivery.

items.title: Construction
items.description: 2–4 Weeks. Demo, plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, tile, vanity, fixtures, and paint.

items.title: Final Touches
items.description: 2–3 Days. Hardware, accessories, glass installation, cleanup, and walkthrough.

items.question: How long does a bathroom remodel take in Orange?
items.answer: Most Orange bathroom remodels take three to eight weeks of active construction, plus material lead time. A hall bath typically takes 3-4 weeks. A full master bath remodel takes 5-8 weeks. Your written proposal includes a complete project schedule that accounts for all lead times.

items.question: How much does bathroom remodeling cost in Orange?
items.answer: Bathroom remodeling in Orange ranges from $8,000 for a half-bath update to $100,000+ for a upscale master suite with upgraded stone, radiant heat, and custom features. A standard hall bath remodel typically falls between $15,000 and $30,000. A master bath with walk-in shower and double vanity runs $30,000 to $60,000. Your written proposal includes a detailed cost breakdown by trade.

items.question: Do I need a permit for bathroom remodeling in Orange?
items.answer: Yes. Most bathroom remodeling projects in Orange require permits from the Orange Building Department, particularly for plumbing and electrical work. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection as part of the project.

items.question: What types of bathrooms do you remodel in Orange?
items.answer: We handle all bathroom types in Orange: half baths, hall baths, master baths, guest baths, and basement bathrooms. Orange's housing stock includes split-levels, colonials, capes, and ranch homes. We're familiar with the construction conditions in these homes and account for them in every proposal.

items.question: Can you convert my tub to a walk-in shower in Orange?
items.answer: Yes. Tub-to-shower conversions are one of our most requested bathroom renovations in Orange. The project typically involves removing the existing tub, modifying the drain location, installing waterproofing membrane, and building a tiled walk-in shower with glass enclosure. Most conversions take 2-3 weeks and cost between $12,000 and $25,000 depending on tile selections and shower configuration.
